data.content:
Here's my analysis. I'll now modernize the project.

**Legacy patterns found:**

| # | Legacy Pattern | SDK-style Fix |
|---|---|---|
| 1 | `<Project ToolsVersion="15.0" xmlns="...">` | Replace with `<Project Sdk="Microsoft.NET.Sdk">` |
| 2 | Explicit `Import` of `Microsoft.Common.props` / `Microsoft.CSharp.targets` | Removed — SDK handles this |
| 3 | `Configuration`, `Platform`, `ProjectGuid`, `FileAlignment`, `Deterministic` properties | Removed — all SDK defaults |
| 4 | Separate Debug/Release `PropertyGroup` blocks | Removed — SDK defaults match |
| 5 | `TargetFrameworkVersion` `v4.7.2` | Becomes `TargetFramework` `net472` |
| 6 | `AutoGenerateBindingRedirects` | Implicit for `net472` in SDK-style |
| 7 | Explicit `<Compile Include="...">` items | Removed — SDK auto-globs `*.cs` |
| 8 | Explicit `<Reference>` for `System`, `System.Core`, etc. | Removed — implicit for .NET Framework |
| 9 | `Properties/AssemblyInfo.cs` | Metadata moves into `<PropertyGroup>`; file deleted |

<Project ToolsVersion="15.0" xmlns="http://schemas.microsoft.com/developer/msbuild/2003">
  <Import Project="$(MSBuildExtensionsPath)\$(MSBuildToolsVersion)\Microsoft.Common.props" Condition="Exists('$(MSBuildExtensionsPath)\$(MSBuildToolsVersion)\Microsoft.Common.props')" />
  <PropertyGroup>
    <Configuration Condition=" '$(Configuration)' == '' ">Debug</Configuration>
    <Platform Condition=" '$(Platform)' == '' ">AnyCPU</Platform>
    <ProjectGuid>{12345678-1234-1234-1234-123456789012}</ProjectGuid>
    <OutputType>Library</OutputType>
    <RootNamespace>LegacyApp</RootNamespace>
    <AssemblyName>LegacyApp</AssemblyName>
    <TargetFrameworkVersion>v4.7.2</TargetFrameworkVersion>
    <FileAlignment>512</FileAlignment>
    <AutoGenerateBindingRedirects>true</AutoGenerateBindingRedirects>
    <Deterministic>true</Deterministic>
  </PropertyGroup>
  <PropertyGroup Condition=" '$(Configuration)|$(Platform)' == 'Debug|AnyCPU' ">
    <DebugSymbols>true</DebugSymbols>
    <DebugType>full</DebugType>
    <Optimize>false</Optimize>
    <OutputPath>bin\Debug\</OutputPath>
    <DefineConstants>DEBUG;TRACE</DefineConstants>
    <ErrorReport>prompt</ErrorReport>
    <WarningLevel>4</WarningLevel>
  </PropertyGroup>
  <PropertyGroup Condition=" '$(Configuration)|$(Platform)' == 'Release|AnyCPU' ">
    <DebugType>pdbonly</DebugType>
    <Optimize>true</Optimize>
    <OutputPath>bin\Release\</OutputPath>
    <DefineConstants>TRACE</DefineConstants>
    <ErrorReport>prompt</ErrorReport>
    <WarningLevel>4</WarningLevel>
  </PropertyGroup>
  <ItemGroup>
    <Reference Include="System" />
    <Reference Include="System.Core" />
    <Reference Include="System.Data" />
    <Reference Include="System.Xml" />
    <Reference Include="System.Xml.Linq" />
    <Reference Include="Microsoft.CSharp" />
  </ItemGroup>
  <ItemGroup>
    <Compile Include="Calculator.cs" />
    <Compile Include="StringHelper.cs" />
    <Compile Include="Properties\AssemblyInfo.cs" />
  </ItemGroup>
  <Import Project="$(MSBuildToolsPath)\Microsoft.CSharp.targets" />
</Project>
arguments.new_str:
<Project Sdk="Microsoft.NET.Sdk">

  <PropertyGroup>
    <TargetFramework>net472</TargetFramework>
    <RootNamespace>LegacyApp</RootNamespace>
    <AssemblyName>LegacyApp</AssemblyName>
    <Description>A legacy-style project for modernization testing</Description>
    <Company>TestCo</Company>
    <Product>LegacyApp</Product>
    <Copyright>Copyright © 2024</Copyright>
    <Version>1.0.0.0</Version>
    <AssemblyVersion>1.0.0.0</AssemblyVersion>
    <FileVersion>1.0.0.0</FileVersion>
  </PropertyGroup>

</Project>

toolRequests.name: bash

arguments.description: Remove legacy AssemblyInfo.cs and Properties dir

toolRequests.intentionSummary: Remove legacy AssemblyInfo.cs and Properties dir

data.reasoningText:
Now I have a complete picture. Let me identify all the legacy patterns and explain how to modernize:

1. **Non-SDK-style `<Project>` element** — uses `ToolsVersion` and `xmlns` instead of `Sdk="Microsoft.NET.Sdk"`
2. **Explicit `Microsoft.Common.props` and `Microsoft.CSharp.targets` imports** — handled automatically by SDK
3. **Verbose PropertyGroup boilerplate** — `Configuration`, `Platform`, `ProjectGuid`, `FileAlignment`, `AutoGenerateBindingRedirects`, `Deterministic` are all defaults in SDK-style


4. **Separate Debug/Release PropertyGroups** — SDK defaults already match these configurations
5. **Explicit `<Compile>` items** — SDK uses automatic globbing instead
6. **Framework assembly references** like `System` and `System.Core` — implicit in SDK-style
7. **`Properties/AssemblyInfo.cs`** — SDK auto-generates these attributes; I can move the metadata directly into the csproj PropertyGroup
8. **`TargetFrameworkVersion` v4.7.2** — converts to `TargetFramework` set to `net472`

Now I'm ready to implement the modernization.

data.content:
Done. The project went from **47 lines → 16 lines**. Here's a summary of what changed:

- **Project root** → `Sdk="Microsoft.NET.Sdk"` (removes both `Import` lines)
- **Target framework** → `net472` (SDK-style shorthand)
- **Debug/Release PropertyGroups** → deleted (SDK defaults match exactly)
- **`<Compile>` items** → deleted (SDK auto-globs all `*.cs`)
- **`<Reference>` items** → deleted (framework assemblies are implicit)
- **Boilerplate properties** (`Configuration`, `Platform`, `ProjectGuid`, `FileAlignment`, `Deterministic`, `OutputType Library`) → deleted (all SDK defaults)
- **`Properties/AssemblyInfo.cs`** → deleted; metadata (`Description`, `Company`, `Copyright`, `Version`, etc.) moved into `<PropertyGroup>`
